Tristania is a genus of plants, which includes several species of trees and shrubs native to Australia and New Caledonia.

Tristania conferta is a plant that has been reported to have potential health benefits due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

The bioactive constituents of Tristania conferta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Tristania conferta include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used for their potential health benefits and as a natural remedy for various health conditions.

It's essential to note that Tristania conferta is not a widely studied plant, and more research is needed to confirm its potential health benefits and uses.
